content: We can model tool-based memory operations for artificial agents after the prefrontal-hippocampal interaction dynamics of human memory retrieval, where the prefrontal cortex acts as a capacity-constrained active learning controller that only pulls context-relevant memory chunks from the large, unstructured hippocampal long-term store via targeted tool-based queries. This framework resolves the longstanding problem of working memory capacity limits that degrade reasoning performance in modern curious AI agents.
